selected_pages.reason: Directly shows a complete example of a resource class that receives JSON via POST and registers it to DB using UniversalDao — exactly the pattern the user is asking for.
Covers all allowed method argument types and return types for resource classes — directly answers the 'resource class implementation pattern' question.
Describes the minimum handler configuration (7 handlers) required for RESTful web services, including BodyConvertHandler and BeanValidationHandler.
Explains how to configure the Jersey adapter with Jackson2BodyConverter for JSON conversion — required infrastructure for JSON reception.
Explains how @Consumes(APPLICATION_JSON) triggers JSON-to-Form conversion — core mechanism for receiving JSON.
Shows how @Valid on a POST method triggers bean validation of the received JSON form.
Explains PathOptionsProviderRoutesMapping and @Path/@POST annotation usage for URL-to-resource-class mapping.
Covers UniversalDao.insert() and BasicDaoContextFactory configuration needed for DB registration.
